audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar camp purity, restitution, and suspected-adultery ordeal text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for camp removal, restitution plus fifth, priestly portions, jealousy/suspected-adultery ordeal, written curse, bitter water, and outcome language. Strong anti-misogyny, anti-coercion, and anti-reenactment safeguards required.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Command children of Israel that they send from camp everyone afflicted with scale-disease, everyone with discharge, and everyone unclean by dead soul/life.
Male and female you shall send; outside camp you shall send them, so they do not make their camps unclean, in whose midst I dwell.
Children of Israel did so and sent them outside camp; as YHWH spoke to Moses, so children of Israel did.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel: When man or woman commits any of sins/misalignments of humanity by acting unfaithfully against YHWH, that soul/life is guilty.
They shall confess their sin/misalignment that they did; he shall return his guilt in its principal and add fifth upon it, and give it to the one against whom he was guilty.
If the man has no redeemer to whom guilt may be returned, guilt returned to YHWH shall belong to priest, besides ram of atonement/covering by which covering is made for him.
Every contribution from all holy things of children of Israel that they bring near to priest shall belong to him.
Each man's holy things shall be his; whatever a man gives to priest shall belong to him.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel and say to them: Any man whose wife turns aside and acts unfaithfully against him,

and a man lies with her with emission of seed, and it is hidden from eyes of her husband, and she is concealed though she has become unclean, and there is no witness against her and she was not caught,

and spirit of jealousy passes over him and he is jealous of his wife, and she has become unclean; or spirit of jealousy passes over him and he is jealous of his wife, though she has not become unclean,

then the man shall bring his wife to priest and bring her offering for her, one-tenth ephah of barley flour; he shall pour no oil on it and put no frankincense on it, for it is grain offering of jealousy, grain offering of remembrance, bringing iniquity to remembrance.
Priest shall bring her near and stand her before YHWH.
Priest shall take holy water in earthen vessel, and from dust on floor of dwelling-place priest shall take and put into water.

Priest shall stand woman before YHWH, loosen hair of woman's head, and put grain offering of remembrance in her palms—it is grain offering of jealousy—and in priest's hand shall be bitter water bringing curse.
Priest shall make her swear and say to woman: If no man has lain with you, and if you have not turned aside into uncleanness under your husband, be free from this bitter water bringing curse.
But if you have turned aside under your husband and become unclean, and a man besides your husband has given his emission in you—
then priest shall make woman swear with oath of curse, and priest shall say to woman: May YHWH make you for curse and oath among your people, when YHWH makes your thigh fall and your belly swell.
This water bringing curse shall enter your inward parts, to make belly swell and thigh fall. And woman shall say, Amen, amen.
Priest shall write these curses in scroll and wipe them into bitter water.
He shall make woman drink bitter water bringing curse, and water bringing curse shall enter her for bitterness.
Priest shall take grain offering of jealousy from woman's hand, wave grain offering before YHWH, and bring it near altar.
Priest shall take handful from grain offering as memorial portion and turn it into smoke on altar; afterward he shall make woman drink water.

When he has made her drink water, if she has become unclean and acted unfaithfully against her husband, water bringing curse shall enter her for bitterness; her belly shall swell and her thigh shall fall, and woman shall become a curse among her people.
But if woman has not become unclean and is clean, then she shall be free and shall conceive seed.
This is Torah/instruction of jealousies, when a wife turns aside under her husband and becomes unclean,
or when spirit of jealousy passes over a man and he is jealous of his wife; he shall stand woman before YHWH, and priest shall do to her all this Torah/instruction.
The man shall be free from iniquity, but that woman shall bear her iniquity.

translation_notes: Verses 2-4 are camp-boundary instructions in a sanctuary-centered wilderness setting; they must not justify abandoning sick or disabled people.
Restitution in verses 5-10 applies to man or woman and requires confession, principal return, and added fifth.

The suspected-adultery ordeal is an ancient patriarchal jealousy procedure centered on the wife, with no parallel ordeal for a husband. Preserve this asymmetry for critique rather than smoothing it away.
The ritual may function as a controlled priestly procedure that prevents immediate private retaliation, but it remains coercive and must never be reenacted.
Outcome language involving belly/thigh is textually and medically uncertain and requires deep lexical audit.
Verse 31's asymmetry must not be used to excuse controlling, jealous, or abusive husbands.
safety_note: Do not reenact, imitate, prescribe, or spiritually replicate the Numbers 5 ordeal. Do not use it to coerce women, accuse partners without evidence, excuse jealousy, shame infertility, blame survivors, or authorize abuse. Preserve it as a difficult ancient patriarchal legal-ritual text under explicit ethical critique.
